title: Hybrid Analysis Integration Guide
content:
## Overview

Hybrid Analysis is a free malware analysis service powered by CrowdStrike's Falcon Sandbox technology. It provides automated static and dynamic analysis of files and URLs in a controlled environment, generating detailed behavioral reports, network indicators, dropped files, and MITRE ATT&CK technique mappings. Integrating Hybrid Analysis with T1 Agentics enables SOC teams to submit suspicious samples for automated analysis and retrieve comprehensive verdicts directly within their investigation workflows.

## Prerequisites

- **Hybrid Analysis Account**: A registered account at [hybrid-analysis.com](https://www.hybrid-analysis.com)
- **API Key**: Obtain your API key and API Secret from your Hybrid Analysis profile under **Profile > API Key**
- **API Terms**: Accept the Hybrid Analysis API Terms of Service to activate API access
- **T1 Agentics Access**: Administrator or Connect Manager role in T1 Agentics
- **Network Access**: Outbound HTTPS (port 443) connectivity to `www.hybrid-analysis.com`

## Configuration in T1 Agentics

1. Navigate to **Connect > Marketplace** in the T1 Agentics console
2. Search for **Hybrid Analysis** in the Threat Intel category or use the search bar
3. Click **Configure** on the Hybrid Analysis integration card
4. Enter your **API Key** and **API Secret** in the credentials fields
5. Configure default **Analysis Environment** (Windows 10 64-bit, Windows 7 32-bit, Linux, etc.)
6. Set **Auto-Analysis** preferences for file types you want analyzed automatically
7. Click **Test Connection** to verify API access
8. Click **Save** to activate the integration

## Available Actions

- **File Submission**: Upload suspicious files for sandbox analysis in multiple environments (Windows 7/10/11, Linux)
- **URL Analysis**: Submit URLs for dynamic analysis to detect drive-by downloads, exploit kits, and phishing pages
- **Hash Lookup**: Check if a file hash (MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256) has been previously analyzed and retrieve existing reports
- **Report Retrieval**: Get detailed analysis reports including process trees, network activity, dropped files, and screenshots
- **MITRE ATT&CK Mapping**: View which ATT&CK techniques were observed during sample execution
- **IOC Extraction**: Extract network indicators (domains, IPs, URLs) and file indicators (hashes, mutexes, registry keys) from analysis results
- **YARA Matching**: See which YARA rules matched the submitted sample
- **Search**: Search the Hybrid Analysis database using hashes, filenames, or other attributes

## Common Use Cases

- **Email Attachment Analysis**: Automatically submit suspicious email attachments (Office documents, PDFs, executables) for sandbox analysis before allowing delivery to end users
- **Malware Investigation**: When endpoint detection alerts fire, submit the detected file to Hybrid Analysis for detailed behavioral analysis and IOC extraction
- **URL Verdict**: Analyze suspicious URLs from phishing reports or SIEM alerts in a sandbox to determine if they deliver malware or host credential harvesting pages
- **Threat Intelligence Enrichment**: Look up known malware hashes to retrieve behavioral reports, network indicators, and MITRE ATT&CK mappings for enriching investigations
- **Incident Response IOC Extraction**: Submit samples recovered during incident response to automatically extract all associated IOCs for detection rule creation and threat hunting

## Troubleshooting

| Issue | Solution |
|-------|----------|
| **401 Unauthorized** | Verify your API Key and Secret are correct. Ensure you have accepted the API Terms of Service on the Hybrid Analysis website. |
| **Rate Limit Exceeded (429)** | Free API access is rate-limited. Wait for the cooldown period or contact Hybrid Analysis for higher limits. |
| **Analysis Pending** | Sandbox analysis takes 5-10 minutes to complete. Poll the report endpoint until the status changes to `completed`. |
| **File Too Large** | Maximum file size for submission is 100MB for most plans. Compress or split large files if needed. |
| **Environment Unavailable** | The selected analysis environment may be at capacity. Try a different environment or resubmit after a delay. |
